title: Tanker report - Week 32
summary:
Clean
The Middle East Gulf has been lacklustre this week on all sizes. On the LR2's tonnage has been plentiful, TC1 has been tested down again 20.32 points to WS211.56 and TC20 lost around half a million dollars to $4.1m. The LR1's have been relatively steadfast in comparison to their larger siblings and TC5 has held in the mid WS260s all week, with a trip west on TC8 only dropping $70,000 to $3.76m.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The Middle East Gulf has been lacklustre this week on all sizes. On the LR2's tonnage has been plentiful, TC1 has been tested down again 20.32 points to WS211.56 and TC20 lost around half a million dollars to $4.1m.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: VLCC rates have turned around this week with improvements seen across all regions. For 270,000mt Middle East Gulf/China, the rate recovered two points to just shy of WS60 (a round trip TCE of $16,900 per day), while the rate for 280,000mt Middle East Gulf/USG (via Cape of Good Hope) gained 1.5 points to WS34.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: Rates for 135,000mt Black Sea/Augusta fell another nine points this week to about WS170 (a round-trip TCE of $63,500 per day) while for the 130,000mt Nigeria/UKC trip, rates fell a further five points to WS122.5 (a round-trip TCE of $30,200 per day). In the Middle East the rate for 140,000mt Basrah/West Mediterranean continued sliding, dropping three points this week to just below WS63.

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The Mediterranean market slipped further, losing about 17% this week. The rate for 80,000mt Ceyhan/West Mediterranean fell 37 points to the WS175 mark (a round-trip TCE of $37,500 per day).
